name: Shrewsbury Cakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the cake flour, baking soda, and ginger.
2. Cream the shortening and sugar until light and fluffy.
3. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
4. Stir in milk or cream.
5. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, mixing thoroughly.
6. Stir in the grated orange or lemon rind.
7. Chill the dough overnight if possible.
8. If the dough is too soft to roll out, refrigerate it for a short time until firm enough to handle.
9. Roll the dough out on a lightly floured surface to about 1/4 inch thickness.
10. Cut into desired shapes using a floured cutter.
11. Place the cut pieces onto an oiled baking sheet.
12. Sprinkle with sugar and caraway seeds.
13. Bake in a hot oven (425°F) for about 10 minutes, or until golden brown.
WARNING: Ensure all steps are executed gently to avoid overworking the dough and causing it to become tough.
